title: Masroor Temples
tagline: Masroor Temples — spiritual town of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: The Masrur Temples, also referred to as Masroor Temples or Rock-cut Temples at Masrur, is an early 8th-century complex of rock-cut Hindu temples in the Kangra Valley of Beas River
overview.description: The Masrur Temples, also referred to as Masroor Temples or Rock-cut Temples at Masrur, is an early 8th-century complex of rock-cut Hindu temples in the Kangra Valley of Beas River in Himachal Pradesh, India. The temples face northeast, towards the Dhauladhar range of the Himalayas. They are a version of North Indian Nagara architecture style, dedicated to Shiva, Vishnu, Devi and Saura traditions of Hinduism, with its surviving iconography likely inspired by a henotheistic framework. topPlaces.name: Sakri, Kangra
topPlaces.description: Sakri is a village located in Fatehpur Tehsil of Kangra district in Himachal Pradesh, India. Rehan is the nearest town to this village.

topPlaces.name: Nagrota Surian
topPlaces.description: Nagrota Surian is a town in Nagrota Surian Mandal, Kangra District, in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Nagrota Surian is 45.2 km from the district headquarters Dharamshala.

topPlaces.name: Maharana Pratap Sagar
topPlaces.description: Maharana Pratap Sagar, also known as Pong Reservoir or Pong Dam Lake, is a large reservoir in Fatehpur, Jawali and Dehra tehsil of Kangra district of the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. It was created in 1975, by building the highest earthfill dam in India on the Beas River in the wetland zone of the Siwalik Hills.

topPlaces.name: Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Pong Dam Wild Life Sanctuary is a sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India. It covers 673 square kilometers.

topPlaces.name: Haripur Guler
topPlaces.description: Haripur is a township in Kangra district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Haripur and Guler are twin townships carrying the heritage of Guler Riyasat.

topPlaces.name: Guler chieftaincy
topPlaces.description: Guler was a minor chieftaincy in the Lower Himalayas. Its capital was the town of Haripur Guler, in modern-day Himachal Pradesh.

It was created in 1975, by building the highest earthfill dam in India on the Beas River in the wetland zone of the Siwalik Hills.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Masroor Temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Masroor Temples is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Masroor Temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Amritsar Junction (130 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Masroor Temples?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Masroor Temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Masroor Temples?
faq.a: Stays in Masroor Temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Masroor Temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sakri, Kangra, Nagrota Surian, Maharana Pratap Sagar, Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary, Haripur Guler.
